objective: Profile and reduce Regular05 live producer compute/scheduling cost while keeping the accepted C++ EGLImage consumer, FIFO handoff, matrix convention, and quality boundaries fixed.

accepted_dependency.consumer_result: Accepted C++ EGLImage consumer and FIFO path are healthy; live_delay90 has rc=0, fallback=0, mismatch=0, black-border p95 below 1%, but 180-frame wall time is about 68.7s.
accepted_dependency.quality_reference: fixed/offline-LP/delay90 source_to_dest Regular05 matrices through the accepted EGLImage path

problem_statement: The current live_delay90 run is much slower than precomputed CSV replay. Existing producer logs report estimate_ms and row write time, but not the LP smoothing and matrix-generation stages that likely dominate wall time.
frozen_variables: producer semantics: bounded_delay_lp_rigid with explicit producer_delay_frames unless this contract declares a single-variable alternative
consumer path: accepted C++ MMAPI/VPI/NVENC EGLImage-wrapper sample
do not use rejected pitch-pointer wrapper
do not use rejected EGLImage image-wrapper reuse
do not use direct mismatched NvBuffer input
do not use block-linear VPI scratch pair
do not use pitch-linear main encoder chain
do not claim full real-time EIS or zero-copy full chain
allowed_changes: optional producer timing instrumentation that does not alter matrix values
bounded-delay producer compute caching or scheduling changes
diagnostic timing-summary scripts or CSV outputs

candidate_sequence.title: Producer timing instrumentation
success_criteria: live_matrix_producer.py can emit a timing-summary CSV
default matrix output remains unchanged when timing-summary is not used
timing separates decode, grayscale, motion estimation, LP solve, mask safety, matrix write, and total elapsed time

candidate_sequence.title: Full Regular05 producer profiling
success_criteria: Regular05 180-frame bounded-delay profiling produces timing_summary.csv
LP solve calls and prefix-frame workload are recorded
the dominant stage is identified before optimization

candidate_sequence.title: Single-variable producer optimization
success_criteria: one optimization materially reduces producer wall time or dominant-stage time
output matrix row count stays unchanged
matrix convention remains source_to_dest
quality/no-regression checks are run before stronger claims

candidate_sequence.title: Jetson same-input verification
success_criteria: optimized producer runs on Jetson or a blocker is classified
consumer/FIFO hard gates remain rc=0, fallback=0, mismatch=0
black-border p95 remains below 0.01
review copy is placed under the categorized review tree
latency table distinguishes producer, handoff, VPI warp, device stage, and wall time

candidate_sequence.title: Power/perf and closeout
success_criteria: power/perf evidence is collected if Jetson access is available
README, one-page summary, evidence map, active contracts, and long-term context are updated only with measured facts
git status is reviewed and results/raw/review videos remain untracked

dryrun_20_frames.interpretation: LP prefix solving dominates even in a small dry run; previous producer_elapsed_us did not include this stage.

jetson_live_stride5_fifo.interpretation: Concurrent live stride5 preserves consumer hard gates and reduces Regular05 180-frame wall time from about 68.7s to 17.5s, but first-frame latency remains high because the producer solves the delayed matrix sequence before frame 1 is delivered.

success_criteria: producer bottleneck is attributed with stage timing rather than guessed
optimized producer preserves matrix convention and row alignment
optimized output passes consumer/FIFO correctness gates on Jetson or a clear blocker is reported
no rejected device route is revived
claims remain scoped to Regular05 unless five-clip evidence is collected
stop_reasons: Jetson SSH or runtime blocker prevents required same-input verification
dominant cost requires a broad producer rewrite outside this contract
optimization changes matrix quality or visual output beyond acceptable no-regression boundary
producer scheduling cannot improve without an unacceptable latency or quality trade-off
technical decision requires internal industrial EIS guidance
knowledge_recovery_policy: first inspect and fix local code
then read docs/knowledge_base/routing.md and at most two relevant local source cards
then search public official/open-source references if the local knowledge base does not resolve the blocker
if still blocked, stop and report in chat with a focused internal-AI prompt
